content_hints.description: Handler Vex's wagon. Walls covered in sketches of light-shapes, attempts to draw the creature's shifting forms. His hands shake. His eyes are wrong. He's been sleeping here since the symptoms started.
content_hints.read_aloud: The drawings cover every surface -- light patterns, geometric shapes that don't resolve into anything solid. Vex sits in the center of them like a man who has been drowning slowly and drawn maps of the water. 'It talks to me in pictures. It shows me where it came from -- a place of light, no ground, no sky. It was happy there. Then the cave. Then the cage. It knows the lock is failing. It's just waiting.'

npcs.name: Handler Vex
npcs.dialogue: I can ask it to wait -- through the link, I can tell it help is coming. But I need you to mean it. It knows when I believe what I'm saying. If you're going to tell me it'll be free by morning, you need to actually make that true. And I know where there's a thinning. The old lighthouse on the coast, two miles. It called to the creature before we even arrived in Newfaire.
npcs.notes: He/him. The investigators' best asset. Can act as psychic intermediary if they commit to releasing the creature. Knows about the coastal lighthouse thinning. Needs medical treatment -- Dr. Asher can stabilize him for the duration. Without treatment, he'll lose the link.

event_triggers: Vex can calm the creature and guide it to the lighthouse thinning -- but only if the investigators commit to release by dawn.
REVEAL: Sustained tones calm the creature. The orchestra's percussion is the worst possible soundtrack. A violin, singing bowl, or sustained vocal note would work.

loot.name: Vex's Light Sketches
loot.description: Forty drawings of the creature's light patterns. A Candela scholar could decode them as a partial language -- the creature has been communicating its history through Vex's drawings without either of them realizing it.

content_hints.description: The animal trainer's equipment tent. Among the standard sedatives and chains, an anomaly: a brass resonance chamber the size of a hat box, old, pre-dating Bellamy's management. Aldo's equipment.
content_hints.read_aloud: The resonance chamber is Candela-made -- the same manufacture as standard investigator equipment. It produces a sustained low tone. Among Aldo's wagon papers, a note: 'Resonance chamber, bleed fauna pacification, range 30 feet, two hours battery. They follow the tone like moths to a lamp. Do not cage them. Invite them.'
event_triggers: REVEAL: The resonance chamber is the key. It calms the creature and can guide it. Aldo knew about bleed fauna and left this tool for exactly this situation.

loot.name: Aldo's Resonance Chamber
loot.description: Candela-issue bleed fauna pacification device. Produces a sustained low tone that calms ambient bleed creatures. Range: thirty feet. Battery: approximately two hours -- exactly the duration of the show.

loot.name: Aldo's Thinning Map
loot.description: Three documented thinning sites within fifty miles of Newfaire, mapped over twenty years. The nearest is the old coastal lighthouse, two miles from the fairgrounds.

content_hints.investigation_success: Aldo's private files, folded inside the chamber's case, describe a protocol for returning a captured bleed creature to a thinning: calm it with the resonance chamber, open a path to the nearest thinning, maintain the sustained tone during transport. The creature follows the tone like a lantern in the dark.

content_hints.description: The medical tent. Dr. Asher's domain. Three roustabouts with radiation burns lie on cots. Through the tent door, the old coastal lighthouse is visible on the skyline -- dark, decommissioned, two miles along the cliff.
content_hints.read_aloud: Dr. Asher is treating a handler's burns when you arrive. 'The radiation output spikes during emotional moments -- applause, laughter, fear. The audience itself will amplify the creature's distress. You need to cancel the show, or failing that, have a containment plan ready when the creature escapes. Because it will escape. The cage lock has maybe an hour.'

npcs.name: Dr. Asher
npcs.dialogue: I can stabilize Vex for long enough to guide the creature. After that he'll need twenty-four hours of rest. I can also triage affected audience members during the incident -- I've got enough sedatives for thirty people. What I cannot do is contain a bleed creature. That's your department.
npcs.notes: They/them. Will treat Vex and prepare for mass-casualty triage. Has emergency kit with sedatives, bandages, and bleed-exposure treatment. Confirms the lighthouse has historical anomaly records consistent with a thinning.

event_triggers: The plan is assembled: Vex guides, resonance chamber calms, lighthouse thinning is the destination. Dr. Asher handles casualties. Crane handles the crowd. The investigators execute.
